raw_output:
Well partner, first things first - you gotta make sure that hoss and you are on friendly terms. Ain't no use climbin' aboard if he ain't comfortable with ya. Once you've got his trust, it's time to saddle up. That leather rig helps keep both of us steady in the saddle.

Next, I always tie my trusty bandana 'round my neck, so I don't choke on dust or sweat. Then, I swing that leg over and sit myself right down in that leather seat like it was a good chair at home.

Now, this is where the real trick comes in - you gotta find yer balance. Both feet are in stirrups, but one foot's a bit further forward than the other, which helps keep me steady when we start movin'. And remember, hoss don't like sudden moves, so always take it slow at first.

Once I got my balance, I give him a gentle pat on his neck and let 'em know we're ready to ride. With a flick of the reins, we set off, galloping across the open prairie, just like a couple of old friends enjoying the sunset together.
